_meta.purpose: Crosswalk from messy free-text 'Living landscape' registry values to controlled MOSAIC living-landscape CODES. Codes follow the existing frontend scheme COUNTRY3-XX (ISO3 + 2-char suffix). Feeds frontend datasets.json 'living_landscape' (CODE) and STAC 'mosaic:living_landscape'.
_meta.code_scheme: COUNTRY3-XX. COUNTRY3 = ISO 3166-1 alpha-3. XX = short mnemonic for the sub-national landscape, or a multi-country basin prefix (MEK for Mekong 3S). UNSPEC/UNK reserved for non-landscape or unresolvable values.
_meta.status: PROVISIONAL - awaiting Lizeth's canonical MFL living-landscape list. Do NOT publish these names as authoritative; they are interim locators. When the canonical list lands, this file is the single place to reconcile.
_meta.match_rule: Engineer: trim whitespace and lowercase before matching free_text. Several free_text variants map to one code (see Zimbabwe Mbire). Unmatched values -> COUNTRY3-UNK if country known, else GLB-UNSPEC, and flag for review.
_meta.checked: github.com/CGIAR-Climate-Data-Hub/metadata vocab/geography.json (UN M49) confirmed 2026-06-22; geography for Country is UN M49, but living_landscape is MOSAIC-specific (mosaic:living_landscape) with no CDH equivalent.

landscapes.free_text: Central India highlands: Hot sub-humid rainfed transition zone
landscapes.name: Central India Highlands

landscapes.free_text: In the Senegal region, the resolution does not allow for data at the departmental level
landscapes.name: Fatick Department
landscapes.note: Sentence is a resolution caveat, not a place. Treated as the Senegal/Fatick landscape; the caveat text belongs in description. FLAG for engineer to verify against the record's actual area.

landscapes.name: Mbire / Lower Zambezi Valley
landscapes.note: Grouped with Mbire variants - see grouping note.

landscapes.name: Northwestern Tunisia
landscapes.note: Source has trailing space ('Northwestern Tunisia '); match after trim.

landscapes.name: Unspecified / multi-scale
landscapes.note: Not a landscape; a scale descriptor. Map to UNSPEC and FLAG - engineer should infer the real landscape from the record's Country/Description if possible.

landscapes.name: Unspecified / malformed
landscapes.note: Malformed row (Country/MFL theme/Data type all = 'Soil dataset'). Keep record, set living_landscape=GLB-UNSPEC, country=null, and FLAG for manual repair.

expected_codes_to_confirm_from_full_data.name: Colombia landscape - confirm sub-national value from full registry
expected_codes_to_confirm_from_full_data.note: Colombia records exist; engineer must read the LL free_text and add the entry. If only country-level, use COL-NAT. If it matches the demo COL-AM (Amazon), reuse COL-AM.

expected_codes_to_confirm_from_full_data.name: Peru landscape - confirm sub-national value from full registry
expected_codes_to_confirm_from_full_data.note: Peru records exist; engineer must read the LL free_text and add the entry. If only country-level, use PER-NAT.

grouping_decisions.ZWE-MB: All four Zimbabwe variants ('Lower Zambezi Valley', 'Mbire Ward 2', 'Mbire: Wards 2 and 3', 'Mbire: Wards 2, 3, 9, 12 and 17') collapse to ONE landscape code ZWE-MB. Rationale: they are nested ward selections inside the same Mbire / Lower Zambezi programme landscape - the differences are analysis extents within one place, not different landscapes. For v1 (functional, not perfect) one code keeps the map and filters clean. Ward-level detail, if needed later, belongs in spatial_resolution / description, not in a new landscape code.

reserved_codes.GLB-UNSPEC: Value present but not a resolvable landscape (scale descriptors, malformed rows).
reserved_codes.{ISO3}-NAT: Whole-country extent (e.g. KEN-NAT). Use when the record genuinely covers the entire country.
reserved_codes.{ISO3}-UNK: Country known but sub-national landscape unresolved.
